questions.question: Explain the Cartesian plane with a diagram.
questions.answer: The Cartesian plane is formed by two perpendicular number lines called the x-axis and y-axis. Their intersection point is called the origin. The plane is divided into four quadrants.

questions.question: Explain coordinate axes and their importance.
questions.answer: Coordinate axes consist of the x-axis and y-axis which are perpendicular to each other. They help locate points in the coordinate plane using ordered pairs.

questions.question: Explain the concept of origin in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: The origin is the point where the x-axis and y-axis intersect. Its coordinates are (0,0) and it serves as the reference point for locating all other points.

questions.question: Explain what an ordered pair represents.
questions.answer: An ordered pair (x,y) represents the position of a point in the coordinate plane where x indicates the horizontal distance and y indicates the vertical distance.

questions.question: Explain the terms abscissa and ordinate.
questions.answer: Abscissa refers to the x-coordinate of a point and ordinate refers to the y-coordinate of a point in the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain the four quadrants in the Cartesian plane.
questions.answer: The coordinate axes divide the plane into four quadrants: I, II, III and IV. Each quadrant has a specific combination of positive and negative coordinates.

questions.question: Explain the sign of coordinates in each quadrant.
questions.answer: Quadrant I has (+,+), Quadrant II has (-,+), Quadrant III has (-,-), and Quadrant IV has (+,-).

questions.question: Explain how points are located in the Cartesian plane.
questions.answer: Points are located using ordered pairs (x,y). The x-coordinate gives the horizontal distance from the y-axis and the y-coordinate gives the vertical distance from the x-axis.

questions.question: Explain why the point (0,5) lies on the y-axis.
questions.answer: A point lies on the y-axis if its x-coordinate is zero. In (0,5), x = 0 so it lies on the y-axis.

questions.question: Explain why the point (7,0) lies on the x-axis.
questions.answer: A point lies on the x-axis if its y-coordinate is zero. In (7,0), y = 0 so it lies on the x-axis.

questions.question: Explain the coordinates of points on the x-axis.
questions.answer: Points on the x-axis have coordinates of the form (x,0) where the y-coordinate is zero.

questions.question: Explain the coordinates of points on the y-axis.
questions.answer: Points on the y-axis have coordinates of the form (0,y) where the x-coordinate is zero.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the origin in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: The origin acts as the reference point for measuring horizontal and vertical distances used to locate points in the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain how quadrants are numbered in the Cartesian plane.
questions.answer: Quadrants are numbered anticlockwise starting from the top-right region as Quadrant I, followed by II, III and IV.

questions.question: Explain why points on the axes do not belong to any quadrant.
questions.answer: Points on the axes have either x = 0 or y = 0 and lie exactly on the boundary between quadrants.

questions.question: Describe the position of a point whose coordinates are both positive.
questions.answer: A point with both coordinates positive lies in the first quadrant.

questions.question: Describe the position of a point whose coordinates are both negative.
questions.answer: A point with both coordinates negative lies in the third quadrant.

questions.question: Describe the position of a point with negative x and positive y.
questions.answer: Such a point lies in the second quadrant.

questions.question: Describe the position of a point with positive x and negative y.
questions.answer: Such a point lies in the fourth quadrant.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between the axes and quadrants.
questions.answer: The x-axis and y-axis divide the Cartesian plane into four quadrants.

questions.question: Explain the importance of ordered pairs in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Ordered pairs help represent the exact position of points in the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain why coordinate geometry is useful in mathematics.
questions.answer: Coordinate geometry helps represent geometric shapes and points using numbers and algebra.

questions.question: Explain the role of the x-axis in locating points.
questions.answer: The x-axis measures horizontal distance of points from the y-axis.

questions.question: Explain the role of the y-axis in locating points.
questions.answer: The y-axis measures vertical distance of points from the x-axis.

questions.question: Explain the contribution of René Descartes to coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: René Descartes introduced the Cartesian coordinate system which connects algebra and geometry.
